title: Secondary indexes do not replace partition key design
description: Native secondary indexes scan all nodes and scale poorly, so they are not a substitute for modelling the right table.
body: Reference for index expectations. A Cassandra secondary index is local to each node, so a query using it must contact every node in the datacenter (a scatter-gather), which does not scale like a relational secondary index. Use when tempted to add an index to support a new query. Do not rely on a secondary index for a high-cardinality or hot-path predicate; it works only for low-cardinality fields within an already-narrowed partition. Validation signals include tracing showing the index query fanning out to all nodes. Risk boundary: a secondary index on a high-cardinality column is a known anti-pattern that degrades the cluster, so prefer a denormalized lookup table or a materialized view and review any new index.
